name: Flood Relief & Distribution
description: Protocols for flood emergency response including evacuation, relief distribution, and post-flood recovery operations.

documents.title: Flood Relief Distribution Protocol
documents.content:
FLOOD RELIEF DISTRIBUTION PROTOCOL

1. DISTRIBUTION POINT SETUP
- Select elevated ground, minimum 500 feet from waterway
- Ensure vehicle access for supply trucks
- Establish perimeter with barricades or tape
- Set up registration desk at entrance
- Deploy shade structures and seating if available

2. PRIORITIZED DISTRIBUTION
- Priority 1: Medical supplies and first aid
- Priority 2: Clean drinking water (1 gallon/person/day minimum)
- Priority 3: Emergency food rations
- Priority 4: Blankets, tarps, and shelter materials
- Priority 5: Hygiene kits and sanitation supplies

3. ACCOUNTABILITY
- Register all recipients with name, household size, and needs
- Photograph or scan identification if available
- Issue distribution cards for tracking
- Maintain daily inventory logs
- Report shortages to logistics coordinator immediately

4. SAFETY WARNINGS
- Never walk or drive through floodwater (6 inches can knock you down, 2 feet can float a vehicle)
- Beware of downed power lines near water
- Watch for displaced wildlife including snakes
- Do not use flood-damaged electrical equipment
- Boil or treat all water from unknown sources
- Wear protective boots and gloves at all times
